title: Group of 200 Indian tourists home-bound after evacuating UAE
byline: A group of 200 Indian tourists has managed to evacuate the United Arab Emirates after a trip to visit the country’s Hindu temples left them stranded as the war broke out.

title: US will have “complete and total dominance” over Iranian airspace soon, White House says
byline: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said Wednesday that the United States will have “complete and total dominance” over Iranian airspace in the next few hours.

title: Qatar evacuating residents “in vicinity” of US Embassy
byline: Authorities in Qatar are evacuating residents living “in the vicinity” of the US Embassy as a “temporary precautionary measure,” the interior ministry said.

title: FM holds talks with Jordan’s Deputy PM and several regional Ministers over Middle East conflict
byline: Minister of Foreign Affairs Vijitha Hearth has held discussions with several foreign ministers of gulf countries pertaining to the ongoing conflict in the Middle East.

title: Showers expected in several areas after 2pm today
byline: Showers or thundershowers are likely at a few places in Uva and Sabaragamuwa provinces and in Hambantota, Nuwara-Eliya and Ampara districts after 2.00 p.m. today, the Department of Meteorology said.

title: Oil price up nearly 2% in early Asian trade
byline: Oil prices rose nearly 2 per cent in early Asian trade on Thursday (Mar 5), off the back of persistent worries about supplies because of the Middle East conflict.

title: Middle East Conflict: No serious injuries to any Sri Lankans residing in affected countries – Officials
byline: A meeting to review the measures currently in place to ensure the safety and welfare of the tourists and Sri Lankan migrant workers, as well as the next steps to be taken in response to the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, was held this afternoon (04) at the Presidential Secretariat under the patronage of President Anura Kumara Dissanayake.

title: Majority of US Senate blocks effort to force Trump to end strikes
byline: A majority of U.S. senators have voted against a bipartisan war powers resolution seeking to halt the military campaign against Iran.

title: ‘Game of Thrones’ movie in development at Warner Bros.
byline: Warner Bros. is officially developing a new movie set in the world of ‘Game of Thrones,’ according to The Hollywood Reporter.

title: Nepal goes to polls in first general election since government-toppling Gen Z protests last year
byline: Nepalese on Thursday morning (March 5, 2026) started to reach polling booths to cast their vote in crucial general elections, the first since a violent Gen Z-led protest that toppled the K. P. Sharma Oli-led government last year.

title: Drone that struck military airbase in Cyprus was not launched from Iran, UK says
byline: The UK’s Ministry of Defence has ruled out Iran as the source of the drone that struck RAF Akrotiri in Cyprus on Sunday night, though it said the unmanned aircraft bore the hallmarks of Tehran’s signature Shahed design — the low-cost drones Russia has used extensively against Ukraine, as SkyNews reports.

title: Explosion reported near tanker off Kuwait, crew safe, UKMTO says
byline: The United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations says it received a report of an incident 30 nautical miles southeast of Kuwait’s Mubarak Al Kabeer.

title: Businessman remanded over Rs. 5 mln bribery charges
byline: A businessman has been arrested by the Commission to Investigate Allegations of Bribery or Corruption (CIABOC) over allegations of accepting a bribe amounting to Rs. 5 million.

title: Two Indian nationals arrested at BIA with over 3kg of ‘Kush’ and hashish
byline: Two Indian nationals who attempted to smuggle a consignment of narcotics into the country have been arrested by officers of the Customs Narcotics Control Unit yesterday (04).

title: Iran launches missiles at Israel as attacks in Middle East commence for sixth day
byline: Iran launched missiles at Israel earlier this morning (05) as aerial attacks in the Middle East commenced for a sixth day after an American submarine sank an Iranian warship in south of Sri Lanka and Iran threatened the destruction of military and economic infrastructure across the region.

title: Sri Lanka recovers 84 bodies from Iranian Ship sunk in US strike
byline: The bodies of 84 individuals who were on an Iranian vessel that sank after a US attack in southern waters beyond Sri Lanka’s maritime boundary have been recovered, the Sri Lanka Navy has announced.

title: Donald Trump rates Iran war ‘15 out of 10’
byline: United States President Donald Trump on Wednesday (Mar 4) rated the US performance in the war with Iran as a 15 on a scale of 10, saying the Islamic Republic’s leaders were rapidly being killed, and vowing to push on.

title: Canada PM Carney says he can’t rule out military participation in Iran war
byline: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said on Thursday (Mar 5) that he couldn’t rule out his country’s military participation in the escalating war in the Middle East.

title: FM discusses Middle East conflict with regional Ministers
byline: The Minister of Foreign Affairs, Vijitha Hearth has held discussions with several foreign ministers of gulf countries pertaining to the ongoing conflict in the Middle East.

title: Nalinda Jayatissa named chair of Parliament Committee on Cyclone Ditwah preparedness
byline: Minister of Health and Mass Media Dr. Nalinda Jayatissa has been named the Chairman of the Parliament Committee appointed to conduct a comprehensive study on the lack of preparedness to face Cyclone Ditwah last year.

title: Bribery Commission arrests retired Health Ministry official and businessman
byline: A retired Chief Clerk of the Ministry of Health has been arrested by the Bribery Commission on charges of accepting a bribe exceeding Rs. 20 million.

title: Benjamin Netanyahu ‘fears US and Iran holding secret talks’
byline: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is reportedly concerned the US is holding secret talks with Iran behind his back.

title: Iranian Naval Vessel Attack: 87 bodies recovered and brought ashore
byline: A total of 32 of the rescued crew from the Iranian ship ‘IRIS Dena’ have been admitted to the Galle National Hospital, with one of them in the Intensive Care Unit, Cabinet Spokesman Minister Nalinda Jayatissa stated in Parliament today.

title: Female lawyer sentenced to 12 years in prison for preparing forged deeds
byline: A female attorney, convicted of preparing forged deeds, has been sentenced to 12 years in prison by the Colombo High Court today (05).

title: Another Iran Ship in Sri Lankan waters? Sajith demands clarification from Govt.
byline: Leader of the Opposition Sajith Premadasa today (05) in Parliament that information has been received indicating that another Iranian vessel is near the Port of Colombo within Sri Lanka’s maritime boundary, and that the government must make a clear statement regarding the matter.

title: Lorry driver arrested for assaulting two police officers in Kalutara
byline: A lorry driver who allegedly assaulted two police officers and tore their uniforms has been arrested by the Kalutara South Police.

title: Israel’s main airport reopens
byline: Israel’s Ben Gurion Airport has been reopened after it was shut down five days ago at the start of the joint Israeli and US military strikes on Iran.

title: Qatar Airways to operate relief flights to European cities
byline: Qatar Airways has just announced it will operate relief flights from the Middle East to several European cities on Thursday.

title: India rejects report claiming US using its ports for Iran strikes
byline: India has rejected a report that claimed that the US was using its ports to strike Iran.

title: Power outages reported across Sri Lanka
byline: A technical fault in the electricity supply system between Anuradhapura and Vavuniya resulted in power outages in several areas across the island, according to the Ceylon Electricity Board (CEB).

title: Customs detects over 52kg of narcotics in air cargo sent from Malaysia
byline: Sri Lanka Customs officers attached to a warehouse in Peliyagoda have detected over 52kg of narcotics concealed in assorted food packages shipped from Malaysia through air cargo.

title: Ex-President Ranil expresses condolences over the passing of Iran’s Supreme Leader
byline: Former President Ranil Wickremesinghe visited the Embassy of Iran in Colombo this morning (05) to express condolences over the passing of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, the Supreme Leader of Iran.

title: Another Iranian vessel near Sri Lanka’s Exclusive Economic Zone, Cabinet Spokesman
byline: The second Iranian vessel is also not currently within Sri Lanka’s territorial waters, but is located in the Exclusive Economic Zone close to Sri Lanka’s maritime boundary, according to Cabinet Spokesman Minister Dr. Nalinda Jayatissa.

title: Iran says US tanker on fire in Gulf
byline: Iranian state television says its naval fighters have hit a US tanker this morning in the northern Gulf.

title: 87 bodies recovered after Iranian Ship sinks near Sri Lankan waters following US attack
byline: The bodies of 87 individuals who were on board the Iranian vessel ‘IRIS Dena’, which sank after being attacked by the United States in seas off southern Sri Lanka, have now been recovered.

title: Iran’s internet blackout hits 120 hours
byline: Iran’s internet blackout has now passed 120 hours, with connectivity in the country flatlining at around 1% of its typical levels, according to independent watchdog organization NetBlocks.

title: ASP Rohan Olugala assumes duties as Director of Walana Anti-Vice Unit
byline: Assistant Superintendent of Police (ASP) Rohan Olugala has officially assumed duties today (05) as the Director of the Central Anti-Vice Striking Unit in Panadura.

title: Fisheries Minister’s request to all Sri Lankan fishermen
byline: Fisheries Minister Ramalingam Chandrasekar today (05) urged all Sri Lankan fishermen not to venture into sea areas beyond Sri Lanka’s maritime borders, considering the current situation in the country’s waters and international waters.

title: US committed ‘atrocity,’ will ‘bitterly regret’: Iranian FM on attack on warship IRIS Dena in Indian Ocean
byline: Iran’s Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi on Thursday accused the United States of committing an “atrocity” after a US submarine sank the Iranian frigate IRIS Dena in the Indian Ocean off the coast of Sri Lanka.

title: Ex-SLAF officer files petition challenging Suresh Sallay’s 90-day detention order
byline: A retired officer of the Sri Lanka Air Force (SLAF) has filed a Fundamental Rights petition challenging the 90-day detention order on arrested former head of the State Intelligence Service (SIS), Major General (Retired) Suresh Sallay, under the Prevention of Terrorism Act.

title: Govt considering appointing interim committee for Sri Lanka Cricket – Sports Minister
byline: Sports Minister Sunil Kumara Gamage says the government is considering transferring the administration of Sri Lanka Cricket (SLC) to an interim committee in the future, as part of efforts to address long-standing issues in the country’s cricket administration.

title: US Senate blocks effort to force Donald Trump to end Iran strikes
byline: A majority of U.S. senators have voted against a bipartisan war powers resolution seeking to halt the military campaign against Iran.

title: European countries to deploy military assets to Cyprus after Iranian attack on UK base
byline: Italy, Spain, France and the Netherlands will send naval assets to ⁠protect Cyprus in the coming days, Rome’s ⁠Defence Minister Guido Crosetto told parliament on Thursday.

title: MP claims two coal grinding machines not working at Norochcholai Power Plant
byline: Samagi Jana Balawegaya (SJB) Member of Parliament (MP) Nalin Bandara claims that two coal grinding machines (pulverizers) at the Norochcholai Power Plant have become inoperative.

title: FM Vijitha Herath says Israel’s FM assured safety of Sri Lankans in Israel
byline: Sri Lanka’s Foreign Minister Vijitha Herath says he held a phone call with Israel’s Foreign Minister Gideon Sa’ar today (05) regarding the well-being of over 29,000 Sri Lankan nationals currently residing in Israel.

title: Israel to attack Iran’s underground missile sites in second phase of war: report
byline: Israel’s war in Iran is entering a second phase that will see its fighter jets attacking ballistic missile sites buried deep underground, two sources familiar with Israel’s military campaign said.

title: National Care Policy essential to achieve fair society that values women’s labour and care economy – PM
byline: Prime Minister Dr. Harini Amarasuriya has stated that establishing a National Care policy is essential in achieving a fair society where it values women’s labour and a care economy.

title: UK sending four extra Typhoon jets to Qatar, PM Starmer says
byline: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said on Thursday that four additional Typhoon fighter jets would be sent to Qatar as the conflict in the Middle East escalates, insisting that the UK has the right plan for defence.

title: Property worth Rs. 30 million linked to major organised criminal frozen - Police
byline: The Illegal Assets Investigation Division of Sri Lanka Police has taken steps to freeze a property valued at approximately Rs. 30 million that is allegedly linked to proceeds generated from drug trafficking.

title: Sri Lanka expressed grave concern over escalation of hostilities: FM on phone call with Iran’s FM
byline: Sri Lanka’s Foreign Minister Vijitha Herath has held a phone call with the Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi yesterday (04) to discuss the rapidly evolving situation in the Middle East.

title: WHO says it has verified 13 attacks on health sites in Iran
byline: The World Health Organization (WHO) chief said on Thursday that it has verified 13 attacks on health infrastructure in Iran amid a U.S.-Israeli campaign and it was checking reports that four medics were killed and 25 ⁠others injured.

title: Sri Lanka will remain neutral during ongoing Middle East conflict – President
byline: President Anura Kumara Dissanayake today announced the stance of the Government of Sri Lanka pertaining to the ongoing Middle East conflict.

title: Over 200 onboard second Iranian vessel being brought ashore; ship to be moved to Trinco – President
byline: A total of 208 individuals who are onboard a second Iranian Navy ship “IRIS Bushehr” near Sri Lanka’s territorial waters are being brought ashore to Colombo at present, says President Anura Kumara Dissanayake.

title: President Anura Kumara’s special press conference
byline: President Anura Kumara Dissanayake is currently addressing a special press conference at the Presidential Secretariat on the Middle-East conflict and the recent incident regarding an Iranian navy ship which sank near Sri Lanka.

title: Indian Navy says it responded to distress call of Iranian ship IRIS Dena, deployed aircraft, vessel
byline: The Indian Navy has sent its aircraft and ships to augment the search and rescue efforts for IRIS Dena that was hit by an American torpedo Wednesday while it was on its voyage back to Iran after participating in exercise MILAN and the International Fleet Review in Visakhapatnam.
